lessons.context: Number and Operations: Fractions (Grade 5): multiplying fractions and interpreting multiplication as scaling (5.NF.B.4–6)
Number System and Decimal Operations (Grades 5–6): fluent multi-digit and decimal calculation across every cost figure (5.NBT.B.7, 6.NS.A.1, 6.NS.B.3)
Measurement (Grade 5): converting within a measurement system (5.MD.A.1)
Expressions and Equations (Grades 6–7): representing the cost model with variables and solving real constraints (6.EE.B.6–7, 7.EE.B.3–4)
Ratios and Proportional Relationships (Grade 7): unit rates and multi-step percent problems including markup and margin (7.RP.A.1–3)
Statistics and Probability (Grades 6–7): posing a statistical question, building and reading data displays, and reasoning from a sample (6.SP.A.1–2, 6.SP.B.4–5, 7.SP.A.1)
Life Science (Middle School): the body as a system of interacting subsystems, chemical transformation of food, and sensory signals to the brain (MS-LS1-3, MS-LS1-7, MS-LS1-8)

Physical Science and Engineering Design (Middle School): determining chemical reactions from evidence, and the full engineering-design cycle of defining constraints, evaluating solutions, and analyzing test data (MS-PS1-2, MS-ETS1-1 through 3)

One challenge covers part of the grade-level sequence; the full set of challenges across the year covers all of it. This one centers on sixth-grade Ratios and Proportional Relationships (6.RP.A.1–3) and extends below and above that:
